summary: The Docker-based reproduction authenticates as a low-privilege test user against the Ajenti panel, then uses the /api/filesystem/read/ endpoint with an absolute path to /etc/shadow and the /api/filesystem/write/ endpoint to create a file at an arbitrary location, confirming that the unsanitized path parameter allows full filesystem access as root.
expected_security_impact: An authenticated user with default filesystem:read permission should be able to read /etc/shadow (a file normally restricted to root) via GET /api/filesystem/read/etc/shadow, and with filesystem:write permission should be able to write an arbitrary file to a sensitive location such as /tmp/evohunt-proof via POST /api/filesystem/write/tmp/evohunt-proof.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started the Ajenti panel inside the container, authenticated as the test user, successfully read /etc/shadow through the vulnerable endpoint (response contained root: entries), and wrote a proof file to /tmp/evohunt-proof confirming arbitrary file write capability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks three signals: (1) the /etc/shadow read response contains 'root:' confirming the file was actually read, (2) a proof file written via the write endpoint exists and contains the expected content, and (3) a chmod operation on a test file changes permissions as verified by stat. These distinguish real path traversal from generic errors, authentication failures, or unrelated crashes.
evidence_produced_by_run: The /api/filesystem/read/etc/shadow response body contains 'root:' prefixed shadow entries, proving the file was read as root.
A file /tmp/evohunt-proof was created via POST /api/filesystem/write/tmp/evohunt-proof with content 'EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_WRITE_PROOF' and verified to exist.
The test user was authenticated via the OS auth provider (su-based), confirming the attack requires only PR:L (low privileges).

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
